message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Heat: jjk-v4-2-sprue (₣BE) [racing]
Paddock: .claude/jjm/jjp_uBuE.md

Next: restore-roundtrip-check (₢BEAAE) [rough]

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Mechanical revert + verification. Sonnet-friendly.
  
  ## Locked decisions
  
  Restore the strict round-trip byte-compare check in `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/src/jjri_io.rs` that was disabled during the sprue rename window. The bypass site carries the marker `SPRUE_MIGRATION_BYPASS` — find it via grep, restore the original `if !is_migration_mode { ... }` block, and remove the `#[allow(dead_code)]` from `zjjdr_find_first_diff`.
  
  The verification step: build, then run any state-mutating jjx call (e.g., `jjx_paddock` setter with a no-op note). It must succeed — proving the gallops file is now canonical for the current source attributes and strict round-trip passes.
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  The serde rename attributes themselves (those are the intended permanent state). Any further sprue-rename work (handled by the four batch paces).
  
  ## Done when
  
  - `SPRUE_MIGRATION_BYPASS` marker absent from `jjri_io.rs`.
  - `#[allow(dead_code)]` removed from `zjjdr_find_first_diff`.
  - Build clean.
  - A state-mutating jjx call succeeds without "Round-trip validation failed" — proves the canonical file passes strict validation under the restored check.
  
  ## Discovery hints
  
  `grep -n SPRUE_MIGRATION_BYPASS Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/src/jjri_io.rs`. The original check is preserved verbatim inside the marker comment block.

b1ed6eb9  ₢BEAAD    Edit-pass 2: drop the 7 transitional alias lines from jjrg_Pace.tacks and the six jjrg_Tack fields (ts, state, text, silks, basis, direction). The basis field's historical commit wire-name fully retires here — the previous serde rename = "commit" was replaced by rename = "jjgtn_basis", alias = "commit" in edit-pass 1; this pass drops the alias, retiring the commit wire-name entirely. Round-trip of .claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json fired earlier this session via the paddock's flip-pattern: jjx_alter BE stabled (initially blocked at default 50KB invitatory size guard, gallops bytes legitimate at 6.35MB; resolved by direct git commit 11012a04 with provenance and authorization) followed by jjx_alter BE racing with size_limit=7000000 (committed 756e43a4, succeeded with the spook-fix from notch 99228774 that added size_limit acceptance to jjrfu_FurloughArgs and jjrm_AlterParams). Build clean (release, 20.57s). Unit tests green: 337 passed, 0 failed across vvr/jjk/vof/vvc. The 13 jjtlg_fundus_scenario integration failures are the same out-of-scope environmental set documented in ₢BEAAA/B/C wraps (missing .buk/users/bhyslop/jjfu-{full,nogit,nokey,norepo}/burn.env BURN profiles + localhost station regime not configured). Operator-mediated MCP restart pending; final gallops round-trip verification (zero bare keys; jjgpn_tacks + 6 jjgtn_* keys present in serialized form) deferred to post-restart session.          
99228774  ₣BE       Spook fix: jjx_alter accepts size_limit override. Mid-execution surface during ₢BEAAD round-trip — paddock's flip-pattern recipe assumed jjx_alter accepted size_limit like its peers (record/close/archive/paddock/enroll/redocket/landing/transfer), but jjrfu_FurloughArgs hardcoded vvc::VVCG_SIZE_LIMIT at the persist call. Mirror notch's pattern: add Option<u64> field to jjrfu_FurloughArgs (with #[arg(long)]), thread args.size_limit.unwrap_or(vvc::VVCG_SIZE_LIMIT) to jjri_persist; mirror on MCP side via size_limit field on jjrm_AlterParams and threaded into jjrfu_FurloughArgs constructor in the JJRM_CMD_NAME_ALTER branch. Build clean (release, 21.26s). Separate spook still standing: jjx_open's invitatory commit lacks size_limit override — surfaced when staged gallops content from the failed alter call blocked all jjx commands at the gateway; resolved this session by direct git commit (11012a04). Both fixes are heat ₣BE infrastructure, outside ₢BEAAD's seven-sprue scope.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
614bb4d7  ₢BEAAD    Edit-pass 1: add #[serde(rename = "jjgpn_tacks", alias = "tacks")] to jjrg_Pace.tacks; add #[serde(rename = "jjgtn_*", alias = "<old>")] to all six jjrg_Tack fields (ts, state, text, silks, basis, direction). The basis field's existing rename = "commit" is replaced with rename = "jjgtn_basis", alias = "commit" — historical commit wire-name retires after edit-pass 2 drops the alias. direction keeps skip_serializing_if. Build clean (release, 22.44s). Operator-mediated MCP restart required between this edit-pass and the gallops-mutating jjx_alter round-trip per paddock recipe — deferred to next session.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
bc21cc6e  ₢BEAAC    BUSJGW garrison-w: rewrite ceremony for three-namespace workload identity (Windows user + workload-owned WSL distribution in HKCU\Lxss + inner Linux user), forced by Microsoft's per-user WSL design where admin's distributions are invisible to workload sessions. Mechanism is SSH-as-workload conducted by the privileged orchestrator: place bare authorized_keys, open workload SSH session (real Windows logon with HKCU mounted), run wsl --import from admin-exported seed tarball + useradd + passwd --lock + workload privkey placement inside the session, then replace bare entry with locked-down command= form and validate round-trip. Adds HKLM ProfileList registration for the Windows account (Win32-OpenSSH #1383). Obliterate scope expands across all three namespaces; admin's rbtww-main remains pristine and serves as seed source.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
b7f4c366  ₣BE       paddock curried: robustify round-trip example to flip-pattern (stabled→racing) per spook surfaced in ₢BEAAC                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
828cb1c3  ₢BEAAC    Edit-pass 2: drop the 8 transitional alias lines from jjrg_Heat (creation_time, status, order, next_pace_seed, paces) and jjrg_HeatStatus (Racing, Stabled, Retired). Round-trip of .claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json fired earlier in this session via two jjx_alter calls on BE (stabled→racing, commits 04e71c9b and b839399f) — the heat-status mutation called save() through the freshly-restarted MCP binary carrying rename+alias, rewriting all heat-property keys from bare to jjghn_* form and all heat statuses from bare to jjghe_* form. Now jjrt_types carries only rename, matching the file shape; future loads require jjghn_*/jjghe_* keys (alias = "primed" on jjrg_PaceState::Bridled is unrelated legacy V3→V4 compat, explicitly out-of-scope per paddock). Build clean (release profile, 19.89s). Unit tests green: 337 passed, 0 failed across vvr/jjk/vof/vvc. The jjtlg_fundus_scenario integration suite reports 13 environmental failures (missing .buk/users/bhyslop/jjfu-{full,nogit,nokey,norepo}/burn.env BURN profiles + localhost station regime not configured) — same out-of-scope set documented in ₢BEAAA/B wraps. Verification: zero bare versions of the five property names, zero bare HeatStatus values; 167 jjghn_* keys + 29 jjghe_* values present in gallops file.

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BE
2	
3	## Goal
4	
5	Mint the unprefixed JSON property names and enum values in `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json` so wire-level tokens follow the project's prefix discipline. The kit's `jjrt_types.rs` types are already prefixed (quoins, in MCM terms); this heat introduces the matching wire-side names (sprues — inlays once they follow discipline).
6	
7	## Sprue scheme
8	
9	Three sub-letters after `jj`:
10	
11	- **X** (constant): `g` — Gallops domain
12	- **Y** (table-letter): the carrier struct in `jjrt_types.rs` — `r` for `jjrg_Gallops` (root), `h` for `jjrg_Heat`, `p` for `jjrg_Pace`, `t` for `jjrg_Tack`. For enum values, Y is the carrier of the field that holds the enum
13	- **Z** (role-letter): `n` for property name, `e` for enum value
14	
15	The suffix after the underscore is the Rust field name (for properties) or the lowercase variant name (for enum values).
16	
17	## Migration recipe
18	
19	Per target, two edits with an MCP restart between them:
20	
21	1. Add `#[serde(rename = "<minted>", alias = "<old-wire-name>")]`. Build. **Restart the chat session** so MCP loads the fresh binary. Run a *gallops-mutating* jjx call to round-trip the gallops file through the new wire-names. The robust idempotent pattern is to flip the heat's status: `jjx_alter stabled` then `jjx_alter racing` — guaranteed to fire `save()` regardless of starting state. (Setting status to its current value may short-circuit; a real flip avoids the ambiguity.) `jjx_record` and `jjx_paddock`-setter do **not** call `save()` on `jjg_gallops.json` — only heat/pace/tack mutations do, so only those round-trip.
22	2. Remove the transitional `alias` line. Build.
23	
24	The blanket `#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]` comes off `jjrg_PaceState` and `jjrg_HeatStatus` once explicit per-variant renames replace it. The pre-existing `alias = "primed"` on `Bridled` stays as legacy compat — outside this heat's window.
25	
26	## Batches and order
27	
28	Carrier-isolated batches keep the silks doppelganger (Heat + Tack) and the three Gallops-vs-Heat near-doppelgangers (heats/paces, heat_order/order, next_heat_seed/next_pace_seed) in distinct paces:
29	
30	1. **mint-heat-silks-pace-states** — `jjghn_silks` plus the four `jjrg_PaceState` variants. Proves the recipe end-to-end.
31	2. **mint-gallops-root-sprues** — the four properties on `jjrg_Gallops`.
32	3. **mint-heat-rest-and-status** — the five remaining `jjrg_Heat` properties plus the three `jjrg_HeatStatus` variants.
33	4. **mint-pace-and-tack-sprues** — `jjgpn_tacks` plus the six `jjrg_Tack` properties.
34	
35	Subsequent dockets reference these by silks rather than re-listing targets.
36	
37	## Spec touch
38	
39	JJS0's prescriptive sprue declarations follow the AXLA mechanism: property-name sites (`axr_member` under `axr_record_json`) carry `axd_property` or `axd_positional` with the sprue inlined in body prose; enum-value sites (`axt_enum_value`) carry `axd_string` with the sprue in a `Serialized as <sprue> in JSON.` body line. JJS0 already carries these declarations for the gallops surface; the four mint paces below carry no JJS0 obligation — only the Rust serde renames and gallops-file rewrite. The same AXLA discipline extends across other specs via the foreign-sweep pace, which adds `axd_string` to existing-wire enum-value voicings outside the gallops surface.
40	
41	## Out of scope
42	
43	`.claude/jjm/jjs_studbook.json` (unconsumed by Rust source); the MCP tool wire format (different boundary); the gazette wire format (already prefixed `jjezs_*`); CLAUDE.md ceiling rule edit (completed at groom-time); a memo footnote about the relaxed ceiling (deferred, not blocking).
44	
45	## Done when
46	
47	`.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json` carries zero bare property names from the original 16 and zero bare enum values from the original 7. Build green; tests green.
48	
49	# jjezs_pace ₢BEAAE
50	
51	## Character
52	
53	Mechanical revert + verification. Sonnet-friendly.
54	
55	## Locked decisions
56	
57	Restore the strict round-trip byte-compare check in `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/src/jjri_io.rs` that was disabled during the sprue rename window. The bypass site carries the marker `SPRUE_MIGRATION_BYPASS` — find it via grep, restore the original `if !is_migration_mode { ... }` block, and remove the `#[allow(dead_code)]` from `zjjdr_find_first_diff`.
58	
59	The verification step: build, then run any state-mutating jjx call (e.g., `jjx_paddock` setter with a no-op note). It must succeed — proving the gallops file is now canonical for the current source attributes and strict round-trip passes.
60	
61	## Out of scope
62	
63	The serde rename attributes themselves (those are the intended permanent state). Any further sprue-rename work (handled by the four batch paces).
64	
65	## Done when
66	
67	- `SPRUE_MIGRATION_BYPASS` marker absent from `jjri_io.rs`.
68	- `#[allow(dead_code)]` removed from `zjjdr_find_first_diff`.
69	- Build clean.
70	- A state-mutating jjx call succeeds without "Round-trip validation failed" — proves the canonical file passes strict validation under the restored check.
71	
72	## Discovery hints
73	
74	`grep -n SPRUE_MIGRATION_BYPASS Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/src/jjri_io.rs`. The original check is preserved verbatim inside the marker comment block.
75

file.content:
# jjezs_paddock ₣BE

## Goal

Mint the unprefixed JSON property names and enum values in `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json` so wire-level tokens follow the project's prefix discipline. The kit's `jjrt_types.rs` types are already prefixed (quoins, in MCM terms); this heat introduces the matching wire-side names (sprues — inlays once they follow discipline).

## Sprue scheme

Three sub-letters after `jj`:

- **X** (constant): `g` — Gallops domain
- **Y** (table-letter): the carrier struct in `jjrt_types.rs` — `r` for `jjrg_Gallops` (root), `h` for `jjrg_Heat`, `p` for `jjrg_Pace`, `t` for `jjrg_Tack`. For enum values, Y is the carrier of the field that holds the enum
- **Z** (role-letter): `n` for property name, `e` for enum value

The suffix after the underscore is the Rust field name (for properties) or the lowercase variant name (for enum values).

## Migration recipe

Per target, two edits with an MCP restart between them:

1. Add `#[serde(rename = "<minted>", alias = "<old-wire-name>")]`. Build. **Restart the chat session** so MCP loads the fresh binary. Run a *gallops-mutating* jjx call to round-trip the gallops file through the new wire-names. The robust idempotent pattern is to flip the heat's status: `jjx_alter stabled` then `jjx_alter racing` — guaranteed to fire `save()` regardless of starting state. (Setting status to its current value may short-circuit; a real flip avoids the ambiguity.) `jjx_record` and `jjx_paddock`-setter do **not** call `save()` on `jjg_gallops.json` — only heat/pace/tack mutations do, so only those round-trip.
2. Remove the transitional `alias` line. Build.

The blanket `#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]` comes off `jjrg_PaceState` and `jjrg_HeatStatus` once explicit per-variant renames replace it. The pre-existing `alias = "primed"` on `Bridled` stays as legacy compat — outside this heat's window.

## Batches and order

Carrier-isolated batches keep the silks doppelganger (Heat + Tack) and the three Gallops-vs-Heat near-doppelgangers (heats/paces, heat_order/order, next_heat_seed/next_pace_seed) in distinct paces:

1. **mint-heat-silks-pace-states** — `jjghn_silks` plus the four `jjrg_PaceState` variants. Proves the recipe end-to-end.
2. **mint-gallops-root-sprues** — the four properties on `jjrg_Gallops`.
3. **mint-heat-rest-and-status** — the five remaining `jjrg_Heat` properties plus the three `jjrg_HeatStatus` variants.
4. **mint-pace-and-tack-sprues** — `jjgpn_tacks` plus the six `jjrg_Tack` properties.

Subsequent dockets reference these by silks rather than re-listing targets.

## Spec touch

JJS0's prescriptive sprue declarations follow the AXLA mechanism: property-name sites (`axr_member` under `axr_record_json`) carry `axd_property` or `axd_positional` with the sprue inlined in body prose; enum-value sites (`axt_enum_value`) carry `axd_string` with the sprue in a `Serialized as <sprue> in JSON.` body line. JJS0 already carries these declarations for the gallops surface; the four mint paces below carry no JJS0 obligation — only the Rust serde renames and gallops-file rewrite. The same AXLA discipline extends across other specs via the foreign-sweep pace, which adds `axd_string` to existing-wire enum-value voicings outside the gallops surface.

## Out of scope

`.claude/jjm/jjs_studbook.json` (unconsumed by Rust source); the MCP tool wire format (different boundary); the gazette wire format (already prefixed `jjezs_*`); CLAUDE.md ceiling rule edit (completed at groom-time); a memo footnote about the relaxed ceiling (deferred, not blocking).

## Done when

`.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json` carries zero bare property names from the original 16 and zero bare enum values from the original 7. Build green; tests green.

# jjezs_pace ₢BEAAE

## Character

Mechanical revert + verification. Sonnet-friendly.

## Locked decisions

Restore the strict round-trip byte-compare check in `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/src/jjri_io.rs` that was disabled during the sprue rename window. The bypass site carries the marker `SPRUE_MIGRATION_BYPASS` — find it via grep, restore the original `if !is_migration_mode { ... }` block, and remove the `#[allow(dead_code)]` from `zjjdr_find_first_diff`.

The verification step: build, then run any state-mutating jjx call (e.g., `jjx_paddock` setter with a no-op note). It must succeed — proving the gallops file is now canonical for the current source attributes and strict round-trip passes.

## Out of scope

The serde rename attributes themselves (those are the intended permanent state). Any further sprue-rename work (handled by the four batch paces).

## Done when

- `SPRUE_MIGRATION_BYPASS` marker absent from `jjri_io.rs`.
- `#[allow(dead_code)]` removed from `zjjdr_find_first_diff`.
- Build clean.
- A state-mutating jjx call succeeds without "Round-trip validation failed" — proves the canonical file passes strict validation under the restored check.

## Discovery hints

`grep -n SPRUE_MIGRATION_BYPASS Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/src/jjri_io.rs`. The original check is preserved verbatim inside the marker comment block.

content.text:
## Pace goal

Restore the strict round-trip byte-compare in `jjri_io.rs` (disabled during the sprue rename window) and verify the canonical gallops file passes strict validation under the restored check.

## Heat context

**₣BE jjk-v4-2-sprue** [racing] · 5/7 done · paddock: mint unprefixed JSON property names in `jjg_gallops.json` to follow prefix discipline (the four batch mints landed across BEAAA–BEAAD; this pace closes the migration window).

**Recent work**: ₢BEAAD just dropped 7 transitional aliases for `jjgpn_tacks` + 6 `jjgtn_*` Tack fields (edit-pass 2). Build clean, 337/337 unit tests green.

## Docket (₢BEAAE — restore-roundtrip-check)

Mechanical revert + verification. Sonnet-friendly.

1. Find `SPRUE_MIGRATION_BYPASS` marker in `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/src/jjri_io.rs`
2. Restore the original `if !is_migration_mode { ... }` block (preserved verbatim inside the marker comment)
3. Remove `#[allow(dead_code)]` from `zjjdr_find_first_diff`
4. Build clean
5. Verify: state-mutating jjx call (e.g., `jjx_paddock` setter with no-op note) succeeds without "Round-trip validation failed"

## Name assessment

`restore-roundtrip-check` fits the docket precisely. No rename needed.

## Approach

- grep for the marker, read the bypass site
- Restore the strict block, drop the `#[allow(dead_code)]`
- `tt/vow-b.Build.sh`
- Trigger a save() via `jjx_paddock` setter with a `note` (no content change) to fire round-trip under restored strict check

**Execution strategy**: Sequential sonnet — single file, mechanical revert, but verification requires an MCP restart between build and the state-mutating call (per paddock recipe). Operator-mediated.

Proceed?

44	/// Find first byte position where two byte slices differ
45	#[allow(dead_code)] // SPRUE_MIGRATION_BYPASS — restored by closing pace `restore-roundtrip-check` (heat ₣BE)
46	fn zjjdr_find_first_diff(a: &[u8], b: &[u8]) -> usize {
47	    a.iter()
48	        .zip(b.iter())
49	        .position(|(x, y)| x != y)
50	        .unwrap_or_else(|| a.len().min(b.len()))
51	}
52	
53	/// Encode a firemark string into a case-safe paddock filename path.
54	///
55	/// Each character is prefixed with 'u' (uppercase) or 'l' (lowercase),
56	/// ensuring the resulting filename is unambiguous on case-insensitive
57	/// filesystems (macOS HFS+/APFS).
58	///
59	/// Examples:
60	///   "AG" → ".claude/jjm/jjp_uAuG.md"
61	///   "Ag" → ".claude/jjm/jjp_uAlg.md"
62	///   "ag" → ".claude/jjm/jjp_lalg.md"
63	pub fn jjri_paddock_path(firemark: &str) -> String {
64	    let encoded: String = firemark.chars().map(|c| {
65	        if c.is_uppercase() {
66	            format!("u{}", c)
67	        } else {
68	            format!("l{}", c)
69	        }
70	    }).collect();
71	    format!(".claude/jjm/jjp_{}.md", encoded)
72	}
73	
74	/// Load and validate Gallops from a file
75	///
76	/// Performs these steps in order:
77	/// 1. Deserialize JSON to Gallops struct
78	/// 2. Round-trip validation: reserialize and compare bytes (validates stored format)
79	/// 3. Semantic validation via jjrg_validate
80	///
81	/// Returns ValidatedGallops wrapper that guarantees the data has passed all checks.
82	pub fn jjdr_load(path: &Path) -> Result<jjdr_ValidatedGallops, String> {
83	    // Read original bytes
84	    let original_bytes = fs::read(path)
85	        .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to read file '{}': {}", path.display(), e))?;
86	
87	    let mut gallops: jjrg_Gallops = serde_json::from_slice(&original_bytes)
88	        .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to parse JSON: {}", e))?;
89	
90	    // Detect old-format files: heat_order absent or schema_version absent.
91	    // Old-format files cannot pass round-trip check because BTreeMap serializes heats
92	    // in sorted key order, which differs from original furlough-shuffled order.
93	    //
94	    // Also detect stale next_pensum_seed field (removed in v3.7 — pensum seeds moved
95	    // to officium-local storage). Serde silently ignores the unknown field on read,
96	    // but re-serialization omits it, causing round-trip byte mismatch until the next
97	    // save writes the clean format back to disk.
98	    const PENSUM_SEED_KEY: &[u8] = b"\"next_pensum_seed\"";
99	    let needs_pensum_seed_removal = !gallops.heats.is_empty()
100	        && original_bytes.windows(PENSUM_SEED_KEY.len()).any(|w| w == PENSUM_SEED_KEY);
101	    let is_migration_mode = gallops.heat_order.is_empty()
102	        || gallops.schema_version.is_none()
103	        || needs_pensum_seed_removal;
104	
105	    // SPRUE_MIGRATION_BYPASS — heat ₣BE (jjk-v4-2-sprue): round-trip
106	    // validation disabled during the wire-name rename window so old-format
107	    // gallops files can pass through the loader, get re-serialized into the
108	    // canonical new sprue format, and be written back. Restored — and
109	    // verified against the fully-migrated gallops — by closing pace
110	    // `restore-roundtrip-check`.
111	    //
112	    // Original behavior:
113	    //
114	    //   if !is_migration_mode {
115	    //       let reserialized = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&gallops)
116	    //           .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to reserialize JSON: {}", e))?;
117	    //
118	    //       if reserialized.as_bytes() != original_bytes {
119	    //           let diff_pos = zjjdr_find_first_diff(&original_bytes, reserialized.as_bytes());

# JJK Kit Source

Edits here apply to the kit source only. Do not modify the active installation in `.claude/` or the parent project's CLAUDE.md.
